reasoning: Our searches up to April 2026 have yielded no confirmed news of a Chinese military base being built, expanded, or established in Africa besides the one in Djibouti. Although US intelligence and foreign policy reports often cite strong suspicions and leaked negotiation details — especially involving locations like Equatorial Guinea (Bata port) and Gabon — there has been no actual groundbreaking or official transfer. Furthermore, the US has exerted significant diplomatic pressure to disrupt these potential agreements, likely stymieing rapid progress. Given that the resolution criteria require either a confirmed start of construction, a publicized groundbreaking ceremony, or an official transfer/establishment of a facility for PLA use, the timeline for this to happen between mid-April 2026 and January 1, 2027 is extremely tight. If a deal were imminent, we would likely see more concrete preparations underway. The crowd consensus was 15% in early April, but absent any recent positive progression, a lower estimate of 7% better reflects the dwindling time window and diplomatic friction.
